editions.summary: Weekly newsletter edition captured for 18 July 2026.
The 30 June 2026 review published as a no-material-change edition: the dashboard held its late-June breast-cancer approvals while official-source checks did not justify a new promotion.
The late-June edition captured two breast-cancer regulatory upgrades: a new palbociclib maintenance dossier and the FDA approval of ASCENT-04 / KEYNOTE-D19.
Morning briefing mode launched with the first watchlist-and-systems expansion, giving the dashboard a plain-language front page and explicit room for nonconforming oncology signals.

highlights.title: Gedatolisib + Fulvestrant
highlights.summary: FDA approved gedatolisib with fulvestrant, with or without palbociclib, for PIK3CA-wild-type HR-positive/HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer after endocrine progression, adding a large progression-free-survival gain in a setting where many patients have already exhausted first-line endocrine options.

highlights.title: Galleri-NHS negative screening signal
highlights.summary: This is a major cautionary signal for population blood-based multi-cancer screening. A negative primary endpoint should slow premature adoption and reshape how MCED claims are presented to payers and the public.

highlights.title: No new material dashboard item cleared the bar
highlights.summary: Official-source checks did not surface a post-28 June development strong enough for responsible inclusion.

highlights.title: ASCENT-04 remained the most recent verified approval
highlights.summary: The shortcut rail still pointed to the 24 June 2026 TNBC approval as the latest regulator-confirmed milestone in the dashboard.

highlights.title: Workforce and screening caution signals remained active
highlights.summary: The dashboard preserved system-level warnings and negative screening evidence rather than dropping them on quiet regulatory days.

highlights.title: Palbociclib HER2 maintenance enters the tracker
highlights.summary: The dashboard added a dedicated PATINA-driven treatment record for the 24 June 2026 FDA maintenance approval in HR-positive, HER2-positive advanced breast cancer.

highlights.title: ASCENT-04 completes the conference-to-label path
highlights.summary: Sacituzumab govitecan plus pembrolizumab moved from ASCO evidence to FDA-approved first-line TNBC therapy.

highlights.title: Brand-name search now resolves newer dossiers
highlights.summary: Portfolio aliasing was expanded so users could find the new palbociclib entry through the `Ibrance` brand name.
